Module com.example.battleship
Package com.example.battleship.model.board

Record Class Position

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
com.example.battleship.model.board.Position
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public record Position(int row, int column) extends Record implements Serializable
An immutable board coordinate, expressed as a zero-based row and column pair (0-9 for a 10x10 board).

Implemented as a record for value-based equality: two positions with the same row and column are considered equal and interchangeable, which is exactly the semantics a hash-based "already shot at" lookup on Board needs.

See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • Position

      public Position(int row, int column)
      Creates an instance of a Position record class.
      Parameters:
      row - the value for the row record component
      column - the value for the column record component

  • Method Details

    • shift

      public Position shift(Orientation orientation, int steps)
      Returns the position one cell away from this one in the given orientation, used while laying out a ship's occupied cells.
      Parameters:
      orientation - direction to step in
      steps - how many cells to move (maybe 0)
      Returns:
      the resulting position

    • toLabel

      public String toLabel()
      Spreadsheet-style label for this position, e.g. "C5" (column letter, 1-based row).

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with '=='.
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.

    • row

      Returns the value of the row record component.
      Returns:
      the value of the row record component

      public int column()
      Returns the value of the column record component.
      Returns:
